code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test16-JAN-2025Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, potassium, and chloride, with marked proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, as well as low urine specific gravity. These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely nephrotic-range proteinuria, and possible tubular dysfunction. The low HbA1c and absence of urinary infection markers are noted. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
